title: Dose Escalation of RMC-4630 Monotherapy in Relapsed/Refractory Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: Participant (male or female) ≥18 years of age
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a manageable participant pool and is not related to safety, efficacy, or feasibility.

eligibility.text: Participants who have advanced solid tumors that have failed, are intolerant to, or are considered ineligible for standard of care anticancer treatments including approved drugs for oncogenic drivers in their tumor type
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a population with a high likelihood of treatment response and is related to the study's primary objective.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 to 1
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients are able to tolerate treatment and participate in the study safely.

eligibility.text: Participants in the Dose-Expansion Component must have one of the following genotypic aberrations: KRAS amplifications, KRASG12C (NSCLC), BRAF Class 3, or NF1 LOF (NSCLC and gynecological cancers) mutations
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a population with specific genetic characteristics that are likely to respond to the intervention.

eligibility.text: Adequate hematologic, hepatic and renal function
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from potential adverse effects of treatment.

eligibility.text: Participant able to understand and voluntarily sign the informed consent form (ICF) and able to comply with the study visit schedule and other protocol requirements.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient comprehension and compliance, which are essential for successful trial participation.

eligibility.text: Participants willing to agree to not father a child/become pregnant and comply with effective contraception criteria
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the safety of potential future pregnancies and is related to the study's ethical considerations.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with conditions that could be exacerbated by treatment and is related to the study's safety objectives.
This criterion excludes patients with CNS tumors, which may pose a risk of complications from treatment.
This criterion excludes patients with pre-existing heart conditions that could be exacerbated by treatment.

eligibility.text: Active, clinically significant interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with lung diseases that may worsen due to treatment and is related to the study's safety objectives.

eligibility.text: History or current evidence of retinal pigment epithelial detachment (RPED), central serous retinopathy, retinal vein occlusion (RVO), or predisposing factors to RPED or RVO
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with eye conditions that could be exacerbated by treatment and is related to the study's safety objectives.
This criterion excludes patients with a known HIV infection, which may pose a risk of complications from treatment.

eligibility.text: Active/chronic hepatitis B or C infection
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with active or chronic infections that could be exacerbated by treatment and is related to the study's safety objectives.

eligibility.text: Any other unstable or clinically significant concurrent medical condition that would, in the opinion of the investigator, jeopardize the safety of a participant, impact their expected survival through the end of the study participation, and/or impact their ability to comply with the protocol prior/concomitant therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety by excluding those with conditions that could be exacerbated by treatment.

eligibility.text: Females who are pregnant or breastfeeding
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes women of childbearing potential to prevent unintended pregnancies during the study.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.text: Number of participants with adverse events (AEs)
Number of participants with dose limiting toxicities (DLTs)

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)
